div.head: The Acting United States Representative at the United Nations (Ross) to the Secretary of State
div.p: 1006. [Here follows report of discussion with United Nations Secretary-General Trygve Lie regarding the General Assembly agenda.]

1. China. Lie volunteered the information that he understood through Assistant SyG HooVictor Chit-sai Hoo, Assistant Secretary-General in charge of United Nations Department of Trusteeship Affairs. that China was thinking of adding a GA item accusing Soviet Union of interference in Chinese affairs. Lie expressed very strong opinion that this should be prevented since no useful action could be taken. He understood that Tsiang had consulted Hoo regarding desirability and procedure for handling item. According to Hoo, Chinese want to bring accusation in order to give the world
background of the question and reduce the influence of US White Paper. It is in large part a move to present to American public opinion the Nationalist case. Lie said, in an effort to head this off, he had deliberately raised the matter in yesterday’s meeting of the Assistant SyGs in order to let the Americans know through PriceByron Price, Assistant Secretary-General in charge of United Nations Administrative and Financial Services. and CordierAndrew W. Cordier, Executive Assistant to the United Nations Secretary-General. and let the Russians know through Zinchenko.Konstantin Zinchenko, Assistant Secretary-General in charge of United Nations Department of Security Council Affairs. He said he would do everything he could to dissuade Chinese from bringing matter before GA. We made no comment.
[Here follow details of the other agenda items.]
